items.title: Win cool gadgets we can’t keep because The Verge has ethics
items.summary: When people learn I work for The Verge, they inevitably ask: “Do you get to keep the gadgets?” Now that I film Today I’m Toying With, a video series where I share the joy of tech, I get that question more than ever. The answer is no, we don’t keep them! Our ethics policy is […]

items.title: The best part of Mina the Hollower is how it randomizes the Zelda formula
items.summary: After rolling credits on Mina the Hollower, I did something unusual for me and immediately started a new file. I'm not typically one to replay games right after I beat them. items.title: YouTube is putting AI labels where you’ll actually see them
items.summary: In the wake of Google expanding its AI verification efforts at I/O, YouTube is now finally going to start taking AI labeling seriously. items.title: My eye doctor got my computer prescription wrong, and AI helped me fix it
items.summary: An eye exam produced a good distance prescription and a terrible computer prescription. Here's how AI helped decode the numbers and expose the mismatch.

items.title: The Witcher 3 is getting another expansion, more than a decade after launch
items.summary: The fourth Witcher game may be a ways off, but fans won't be without Geralt of Rivia for long. items.title: Tech CEOs are apparently suffering from AI psychosis
items.summary: "CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis," Box CEO Aaron Levie opines. Maybe that explains the almost religious belief in AI productivity gains.
